metadata.name: molywood
metadata.summary: A script to automate the production of molecular movies in VMD

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.postprocessor
documentation:
This is the key function that controls composition
of the previously rendered scenes. It first applies
overlays if requested, and then copies/moves/composes
individual frames so that an initial set of
scene1...png, sceneX...png files is converted
into movie_name...png files that can then
be merged into a file using ffmpeg.
\:param script: Script instance, the master object controlling the movie layout
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.check_overlays
documentation:
Ensures that dependent overlays (i.e. Scenes
that will be rendered atop other Scenes)
are properly defined, and calculates level
of nesting for subsequent ordering of
rounds of Scene composition
\:param script: Script instance, the master object controlling the movie layout
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.gen_fig
documentation:
Responsible for frame-by-frame generation of files
associated with (a) external images and (b)
on-the-fly generated matplotlib plots
(essentially any graphics that is not rendered
by TCL/VMD)
\:param action: Action, object to extract info from
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.gen_fig_show
documentation:
Generates a static figure whem action is `show_figure`
\:param action: The Action object to draw data from
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.gen_ovl
documentation:
Handles the generation of dynamic overlays (figures,
text, plots, movies) as requested by `add_overlay`
\:param action: The Action object to draw data from
\:param ovl: str, key for the Action.overlays dictionary
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.calc_ores
documentation:
Calculates per-frame overlay resolutions
in case relative size is scaled linearly
\:param action: Action or SimultaneousAction instance
\:param ovl: str, name of the overlay
\:param frames: iterable, enumerates all frames overlay should apply to
\:param default: bool, whether a default should be provided or error raised when relative_size is not defined
\:return: list of 2-element lists: \[[x_res, y_res] for frame in frames\]

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.equalize_frames
documentation:
If individual scenes have different frame counts,
this function appends the corresponding last figure
to the shorter scenes
\:param script: Script instance, the master object controlling the movie layout
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.compose_overlay
documentation:
Introduces the overlay provided that the picture
('overlay-frame_name.png') was already produced
and properly scaled by gen_fig
\:param action: Action or SimultaneousAction, object to extract data from
\:param master: bool, whether to compose with the final frames (for master_overlays)
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.extract_value_actions
documentation:
Takes a list of Actions that are spanned by a given overlay and calculates
a frame-by-frame list of values of a given property (specified by `value`)
so that intermediate Actions can modify the original value
\:param actions: list of Actions, actions affected by the overlay
\:param ovl: str, alias of the overlay
\:param value: str, name/keyword of the property to extract
\:param var_type: type, variable type (to be checked for compatibility of conversion)
\:param default: any, default value if not specified for 1st action
\:return: list of tuples, length equal to num of frames affected by the overlay

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.data_simple_plot
documentation:
Creates a set of customizable 1D line plots
or 2D hexbin plots based on a provided data file
to e.g. accompany the display of an
animated trajectory
\:param action: Action or SimultaneousAction, object to extract data from
\:param datafile: str, file containing the data to be plotted
\:param basename: str, base name of the image to be produced (e.g. 'overlay1')
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.calc_frames_ud
documentation:
If an action can have modes up (u), down (d) or up-down (ud),
this function will match 'ups' with 'downs' to return the
frame numbers and Actions covered by the current action's span
\:param action: Action, contains at least one add_overlay
\:param ovl: str, name of the overlay
\:return: iterator, enumerates frames spanned by the action

> list, contains Actions affected by the overlay

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.process_audio
documentation:
Processes directives for the `add_audio` Action,
yielding a list of relevant variables like starting
poits, segment length, name of the file etc. then
sorts the segments according to their starting times
\:param scr: The top-level Script object
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.call_moly
documentation:
A wrapper to consistently take care of all external calls
\:param arg: str, a command to be run from the command line
\:param answer: bool, whether to return the captured output
\:param log: bool, whether to log the command to a file
\:return: str, captured output from the command

functions.name: molywood.graphics_actions.check_if_convertible
documentation:
Checks if the user-specified value can be converted
to the desired data type
\:param string: user-specified value
\:param object_type: what type should 'string' be convertible to
\:param param_name: name of the parameter in question
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.sigmoid_increments
documentation:
Returns stepwise increments that result in logistic
growth from 0 to sum over n_points
\:param n_points: int, number of increments
\:param abruptness: float, how fast the transition is
\:return: numpy.array, array of increments

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.sigmoid_norm_sum
documentation:
Yields n_points increments that sum to cumsum
\:param cumsum: float, cumulative sum of the increments
\:param n_points: int, number of increments
\:param abruptness: float, how fast the transition is
\:return: numpy.array, array of increments

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.sigmoid_norm_prod
documentation:
Yields n_points increments that multiply to cumprod
\:param cumprod: float, cumulative sum of the increments
\:param n_points: int, number of increments
\:param abruptness: float, how fast the transition is
\:return: numpy.array, array of increments

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.sigmoid_norm_sum_linear_mid
documentation:
Yields n_points increments that sum to cumsum
by first starting with smooth sigmoid increments,
then incrementing linearly, and then stopping
smoothly again with sigmoid increments
(e.g. we want to rotate more or less continuously
by 720deg about an axis, but start and finish smoothly)
\:param cumsum: float, cumulative sum of the increments
\:param n_points: int, number of increments
\:param abruptness: float, how fast the transition is
\:param fraction_linear: float, fraction of the action spent in the linear regime
\:return: numpy.array, array of increments

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.logistic
documentation:
The logistic fn used to smoothen transitions
\:param x: abscissa
\:param k: transition abruptness
\:return: numpy.array, values of the logistic fn

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.logistic_deriv
documentation:
Derivative of the logistic fn used to smoothen transitions
(in a discretized version yields single-step increments)
\:param x: abscissa
\:param k: transition abruptness
\:return: numpy.array, values of the logistic fn derivative

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.gen_loop
documentation:
The main fn that generates TCL code (mostly loops,
hence the name). We mostly use three-letter names for
variables/dict entries according to the convention:
center_view -> ctr;
rotate -> rot;
zoom_in/zoom_out -> zin/zou;
animate -> ani etc.
Functions are being called in the following order:
(1) gen_setup(action)
(2) gen_iterators(action)
(3) gen_command(action)
(4) gen_cleanup(action)
\:param action: Action or SimultaneousAction, object to extract info from
\:return: str, formatted TCL code

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.gen_setup
documentation:
Some actions (e.g. centering) require a setup step that
only has to be performed once; this fn is supposed
to take care of such thingies
\:param action: Action or SimultaneousAction, object to extract info from
\:return: dict, formatted as label: command

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.gen_iterators
documentation:
to serve both Action and SimultaneousAction, we return
a dictionary with three-letter labels and a list of
values already formatted as a string
\:param action: Action or SimultaneousAction, object to extract info from
\:return: dict, formatted as label: iterator

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.gen_command
documentation:
We assume action_type is either a list of strings
or a single string, so that one does not need to care
whether we're dealing with a single action or many;
we return a dict formatted consistently with gen_iterators()
\:param action: either Action or SimultaneousAction
\:return: dict, formatted as label: TCL command

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.gen_cleanup
documentation:
Whenever a TCL action requires some
post-execution cleanup (e.g. deletion
of highlights), this fn generates code
to handle this
\:param action: either Action or SimultaneousAction
\:return: dict, formatted as label: TCL command

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.check_if_convertible
documentation:
Checks if the user-specified value can be converted
to the desired data type
\:param string: user-specified value
\:param object_type: what type should 'string' be convertible to
\:param param_name: name of the parameter in question
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.check_sigmoid
documentation:
Checks sigmoid settings, for most actions
this can be true, false or sls; in principle,
transition abruptness can also be specified
separately
\:param params_dict: a dict that will contain sigmoid parameters
\:return: bool, whether the transition follows a sigmoid

> bool, whether to make the transition sls (sigmoid-linear-sigmoid)
> float, abruptness of the transition (how steep is the sigmoid)

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.get_fraction
documentation:
When user only wants to run a fraction
of the Action (e.g. to split one long Action
between several shorter ones), this fn
will calculate all numerical parameters
\:param action: either Action or SimultaneousAction
\:param action_dict: a dict that will contain fraction parameters
\:return: int, total number of frames (actual will be a fraction of this number)

> float, beginning of the action as numbered by framenum
> float, end of the action as numbered by framenum

functions.name: molywood.tcl_actions.add_once
documentation:
Filters TCL functions to avoid multiple instances
of exactly identical functions in the script
(just for aesthetics, honestly)
\:param function: a Function object that returns the TCL code (see library below)
\:param action: either Action or SimultaneousAction
\:return: str, TCL code

functions.name: molywood.moly.molywood
documentation:
This is the initial entry point for the script,
accessible both from the command line
and when the script is run manually
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.moly.run_script
documentation:
The main entry point for the script, allows
for easy testing with an additional -test
parameter in the command line
\:param input_name: path to the input file
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.moly.gen_yml
documentation:
Generates the .yml file on-the-fly when extra
dependencies need to be installed; this is convenient
as we do not need to re-install existing dependencies
in the new virtual environment, saving disk space.
This fn also installs the venv.
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.moly.check_deps
documentation:
Tests for dependencies, both python libraries
(numpy, matplotlib, seaborn) and other software
(ffmpeg, vmd, imagemagick); returns a list of
missing dependencies and corresponding conda
channels to get them from
\:param printing: bool, whether to print the
results of individual checks
\:return: list of str, missing dependencies

> list of str, channels from which to download them

functions.name: molywood.moly.manual_install
documentation:
Prints suggestions on manual installation of dependencies
if some are missing and cannot be installed automatically
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.moly.gen_example
documentation:
Generates a sample input file to be shown as an example
when 'molywood' is run with no input args
\:return: None

functions.name: molywood.moly.call_moly
documentation:
A wrapper to consistently take care of all external calls
\:param arg: str, a command to be run from the command line
\:param answer: bool, whether to return the captured output
\:param log: bool, whether to log the command to a file
\:return: str, captured output from the command

classes.name: molywood.moly.Script

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.render
documentation:
The final fn that renders the movie (runs
the TCL script, then uses combine and/or
ffmpeg to assemble the movie frame by frame)
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.show_script
documentation:
Shows a sequence of scenes currently
buffered in the object for rendering;
mostly for debugging purposes
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.from_file
documentation:
Reads the full movie script from an input file
and runs parser/setter functions
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.process_loops
documentation:
Converts repeat/end repeat directives (loops)
into repeated snippets of text, defining variables
and substituting them in the reference text
\:param script: list of str, content of the input file
\:return: list of str, processed input

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.setup_os_commands
documentation:
Paths to VMD, imagemagick utilities, OS-specific
versions of rm/del, ls/dir, which/where, ffmpeg etc.
have to be determined to allow for Linux/OSX/Win
compatibility. NOTE: compatibility with Windows might be
a future feature, currently not well-tested
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.parse_directives
documentation:
Reads global directives that affect
the main object (layout, fps, draftmode
etc.) based on \$-prefixed entries
\:param directives:
\:return: dict, contains dictionaries of global keyword:value parameters

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.parse_scenes
documentation:
Reads info on individual scenes and initializes
Scene objects, later to be appended to the Script's
list of Scenes
\:param scenes: dict, contains scene_name: description bindings
\:return: list, container of Scene objects

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.prepare
documentation:
Once text input is parsed, this fn sets
global parameters such as fps, draft mode
or whether to keep frames
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.check_path
documentation:
Looks for the specified file in the local
directory and at the location of the input
file; raises a RuntimeError if file cannot
be found
\:param filename: str, path (relative or absolute) of the file to be sought
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.check_tcl
documentation:
If the files to be read by VMD were saved as
absolute paths and then transferred to another
machine, this fn will identify missing paths
and look for the files in the working dir,
creating another file if needed
\:param tcl_file: str, path to the VMD visualization state
\:return: str, new (or same) path to the VMD visualization state

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.parse_audio
documentation:
Takes care of audio processing in several steps:
first goes through a list of audio pieces to look
for gaps and overlaps, trimming and creating silence;
then parses each input audio file to match length etc.;
finally combines all inputs (including patches of silence)
into a single audio file and adds it to the previously
rendered video file.
\:param total_time: float, total time of the movie (in seconds)
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Script.convert_to_gif
documentation:
When conversion to gif is requested, takes the final
.mp4 files and converts them all to gifs; if parameters
are wrongly specified, shows the ffmpeg command to save
the user some time
\:return: None

Main class that will combine all information
and render the movie (possibly with multiple
panels, overlays etc.)

classes.name: molywood.moly.Scene

methods.name: molywood.moly.Scene.add_action
documentation:
Adds an action to the subscript
\:param description: str, description of the action
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Scene.show_script
documentation:
Shows actions scheduled for rendering
within the current subscript; mostly
for debugging purposes
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Scene.calc_framenum
documentation:
Once the fps rate is known, we can go through all actions
and set integer frame counts as needed. Note: some actions
can be instantaneous (e.g. recenter camera), so that
not all will have a non-zero framenum. Also, the cumulative
frame number (for the entire Scene) will be calculated.
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Scene.merge_after
documentation:
Re-processes frame numberings, scene
names etc. if the current Scene is meant
to follow another Scene (specified by setting
'after=previous_scene' in the Scene input)
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Scene.tcl
documentation:
This is the top-level function that produces
an executable TCL script based on the corresponding
action.generate() functions; also, many defaults are
set here to override VMD's internal settings
\:return: str, the TCL code to be executed

A Scene instance is restricted to a single
molecular system; all Scene parameters are
read as input is parsed.

classes.name: molywood.moly.Action

methods.name: molywood.moly.Action.generate_tcl
documentation:
Should yield the TCL code that will
produce the action in question; in case
of restarting, checks whether frames have
been rendered already for this action
\:return: str, TCL code

methods.name: molywood.moly.Action.check_if_rendered
documentation:
In case of restarting, checks whether frames have
been rendered already for this action
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Action.generate_graph
documentation:
Runs external functions that take care of
on-the-fly rendering of matplotlib graphs
or copying of external images
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Action.parse
documentation:
Parses a single command from the text input
and converts into action parameters
\:param command: str, description of the action
\:param ignore: tuple, list of parameters to ignore while parsing
(these will be stored in special-purpose dicts to avoid interference)
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.Action.split_input_line
documentation:
A modified string splitter that doesn't split
words encircled in quotation marks; required
by actions that accept a VMD-compatible
selection string
\:param line: str, line to be split
\:return: list of strings, contains individual words

Intended to represent a single action in
a movie, e.g. a rotation, change of material
or zoom-in

classes.name: molywood.moly.SimultaneousAction

methods.name: molywood.moly.SimultaneousAction.parse
documentation:
We simply add action parameters to the
params dict, assuming there will be no
conflict of names (need to ensure this
when setting action syntax); this *is*
a workaround, but should work fine for
now - might write a preprocessor later
to pick up and fix any possible issues
\:param command: str, description of the actions
\:param ignore: tuple, list of parameters to ignore while parsing
\:return: None

methods.name: molywood.moly.SimultaneousAction.parse_many
documentation:
Several types of actions have non-unique
keywords or can be defined multiple times
per Action, and hence are specifically handled
by this function to put the params into
a separate dictionary (e.g. self.highlights)
\:param directive: str, input section that defines a single Action in SimultaneousAction
\:param actions_dict: dict, the dictionary to handle the given action
\:param keyword: str, unique name of the action (e.g. 'highlight1')
\:return: None

Intended to represent a number of actions
that take place simultaneously (e.g. zoom
and rotation)
